Find Sherpas Boutique Search Agency
Search Quality Report

62 Cotswold Outdoor Searches. 30 Revenue-Risk Moments.

An independent audit of how Cotswold Outdoor search handles product names, brands, filters, and shopper intent.
Prepared by Michal Pekarcik · Find Sherpas
Report Crossroads

Jump to a section

Search risk at a glance → Tested query patterns → Improvement map → Advanced diagnostics → Prioritized roadmap → Revenue-risk failures → Detailed analysis → Complete test results → Scope and boundaries →
Summary

Search risk at a glance

The short version

We tested 62 realistic customer queries against Cotswold Outdoor's search, live on site. The audit checks whether customers see a relevant top result, not just whether the search engine returns something.

This report focuses on the 30 queries where a customer likely hits a dead end: an off-topic result, a missing interpretation, a dropped constraint, or a relevant product buried too far down.

The first fix to ship: Fix relevance-based ranking across all product categories — Reconfigure the ranking algorithm to prioritize computed relevance scores over secondary factors like brand preference, price, or alphabetical ordering.

48%
revenue risk. 30 of 62 tested queries cause a likely customer bounce today. These are queries where a customer sees a dead end: no relevant top result, wrong interpretation, or the right product buried too far down.
30
Revenue risk
Off-topic top result, missing interpretation, or severe ranking failure. The customer leaves.
32
Friction
The query is close, but still creates work: the best match is not surfaced cleanly enough.
0
Working well
Top result is relevant and ranking is near-optimal. No immediate attention needed.
84%
Right products found, ranked too low
#1
Avg. best result position
0%
Best result outside top 3
26%
Irrelevant #1 result
Most severe example A customer searching "autumn trail running kit" gets zero results — a blank page, nothing at all.
Pattern Calibration Map

Where each query family needs tuning

These are diagnostic probes, not grades. Each row shows the strength of the tuning signal in that sample, separating severe shopper-visible misses from minor ranking polish.

Product Discovery
Direct match
Exact-match polish
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “waterproof jackets” — The highest relevance result 'Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket' (0.852 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-relevance items…
Can customers find an exact product when they already know the name?
Next tuning move: Protect exact product-title matches and verify they are not outranked by broader keyword matches.
Product Discovery
Broad category
Collection routing
4 probes reviewed: 3 visible misses, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “sale” — When searching for 'sale', customers expect to see discounted products, but the engine returned full-price items like Meindl Mens Bhutan MFS GTX Boots at…
Does search understand high-level shopping terms and collection-style queries?
Next tuning move: Map common retail terms to collections, category pages, and merchandising rules.
Typo Tolerance
Typo
Typo recovery
4 probes reviewed: 2 visible misses, 1 friction point, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “slaomon” — The search engine successfully handled the typo 'slaomon' and retrieved all Salomon products, but severely misranked them.
Does search recover when brand or product terms are misspelled?
Next tuning move: Add fuzzy matching for high-value brands, product names, and common category terms.

Typo Tolerance
Partial query
Autocomplete intent
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 3 friction points.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “patag” — The query 'patag' is clearly a partial match for 'Patagonia' brand, but zero Patagonia products appear in the 9 results.
Do incomplete typed-ahead queries surface plausible products before the full term is entered?
Next tuning move: Tune prefix matching and autocomplete-backed retrieval for short or incomplete terms.

Filters & Constraints
Multi-attribute
Constraint stacking
4 probes reviewed: 1 visible miss, 2 friction points, 1 polish note.
At least one probe showed a shopper-visible miss. The example below is the clearest case.
Example “patagonia fleeces sale” — The query 'patagonia fleeces sale' includes 'sale' as a constraint, but none of the returned results appear to be on sale.
Can search keep multiple requirements active at the same time?
Next tuning move: Score each constraint independently so one matched term does not drown out the rest.

Advanced Diagnostics

What the visible result set reveals

These checks stay inside the same query → results → audit boundary, but look beyond the single best result.

Result Set Purity

This checks how much of the visible result set is actually relevant, not just whether one good result exists somewhere. A noisy top 5 means customers must visually filter around weak results even when search technically found a match.

40%
top 5 relevant

Result

The visible result set is noisy: only 40% of top 5 results were relevant on average, and top 10 purity was 42%.

Evidence

  • "accessories": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "sale": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "patagnoia": 0/5 relevant in top 5
  • "gortex jackets": 0/5 relevant in top 5

How to remedy it

Tune ranking to demote low-relevance partial matches once a strong intent match exists. Add a top-N quality guardrail: for each important query class, require a minimum share of relevant results in the top 5, not only a good best-result position.

Top Result Trust

This asks whether the first result is defensible as the best thing to show a customer. A bad #1 is the clearest search failure because it shapes the shopper's first impression.

68%
questionable #1 results

Result

The first result is not reliable enough: 42 of 62 queries (68%) had a weak, partial, or clearly beaten #1 result.

Evidence

  • "autumn trail running kit": #1 was "No results" (score 0.00). No visible result was available.
  • "uv sunglasses": #1 was "Bridgedale Mens Special Edition Merino Hiker Socks" (score 0.21). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "softshelljackets": #1 was "Bridgedale Mens Special Edition Merino Hiker Socks" (score 0.24). The first result is weakly related to the query.
  • "spring sale jackets": #1 was "Bridgedale Mens Special Edition Merino Hiker Socks" (score 0.26). The first result is weakly related to the query.

How to remedy it

Add a stricter first-result threshold for high-intent queries. If the top result is weak and a stronger result exists lower, boost the stronger exact/category/brand match or suppress the weak partial match from rank #1.

Attribute Drift

This decomposes multi-attribute queries to see which terms survive into the top results. It distinguishes right-category-but-wrong-attribute failures from total search failures.

3
queries with attribute loss

Result

3 multi-attribute queries showed some attribute loss in the top 5. The audit separates preserved, partially preserved, and dropped terms so the team can see which part of the query is being lost.

Evidence

  • "salomon walking boots waterproof": dropped walking, waterproof; partially preserved none
  • "men's waterproof jackets": dropped waterproof; partially preserved none
  • "north face insulated jackets": dropped insulated; partially preserved none
  • "patagonia fleeces sale": dropped none; partially preserved none

How to remedy it

Extract query attributes before ranking and give required attributes explicit weight. For brand, size, material, activity, and style terms, penalize results that match the product type but omit or contradict the attribute.

Diversity vs Redundancy

This checks whether broad or exploratory queries show useful choice, or whether the top results are flooded by repeated variants. It is most useful for multi-brand or broad-catalog ecommerce, and less useful for true single-brand stores.

6
unique families in worst broad top 10

Result

The most redundant broad result set was "accessories": 6 unique product families across 10 top results.

Evidence

  • "accessories": 6 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "sock": 6 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "sale": 8 unique product families across 10 top results
  • "fleece tops": 8 unique product families across 10 top results

How to remedy it

Group color/size/variant duplicates under a product-family cap for broad queries. Keep exact product searches variant-rich, but diversify broad discovery queries across families, brands, styles, and use cases.

Recommendations

Prioritized Roadmap

1

Fix relevance-based ranking across all product categories

Reconfigure the ranking algorithm to prioritize computed relevance scores over secondary factors like brand preference, price, or alphabetical ordering.

Critical Quick Win
2

Implement compound word splitting for merged queries

Add logic to split queries like "walkingboots" and "waterprooftrousers" into component words ("walking boots", "waterproof trousers") to match relevant products.

Critical Medium Effort
3

Add semantic understanding for natural language queries

Configure the search engine to recognize phrases like "something waterproof for walking in the rain" and map them to relevant product categories instead of literal keyword matching.

Critical Major Project
4

Create sale status filtering and constraint recognition

Implement proper sale/promotional status indexing so queries containing "sale" only return discounted products, and ensure sale items are prioritized when sale terms are present.

Critical Medium Effort
5

Build category-aware ranking for product type queries

When customers search for specific product types (like "jackets" or "boots"), boost products in those categories over accessories or unrelated items that happen to contain the keywords.

Critical Medium Effort
6

Add seasonal and use-case query expansion

Implement query expansion rules that map seasonal terms like "winter hiking gear" to appropriate product categories (insulated jackets, waterproof boots) rather than literal keyword matches.

Critical Medium Effort
7

Implement brand abbreviation and synonym recognition

Add mappings for common abbreviations like "TNF" → "The North Face" and "UV" → "ultraviolet" to ensure customers can find products using familiar shorthand.

Moderate Quick Win
Industry Context

How You Compare

Metric Your Score Industry Target Source
Relevant result in top 3 100% 80%+ Baymard Institute
Average best result position #1 #1–2 Industry best practice
Irrelevant #1 result 26% <10% Industry best practice

70% of ecommerce sites cannot handle typos in their site search (Baymard Institute). Sites that do handle them gain a significant edge.

~30% of ecommerce visitors use site search, but they generate a disproportionate share of revenue — search users convert at 1.8–3x the rate of browse-only visitors (Econsultancy, Algolia).

31% of site searches end at a dead end — irrelevant results or zero results — across the average ecommerce site (Baymard Institute).

The top 3 results capture ~55–60% of all clicks. Results below position 3 are seen by a rapidly shrinking share of customers.

Revenue-Risk Failures

The queries most likely to cost sales

These examples show where the customer either sees no useful result, sees the wrong intent first, or has to dig past weaker results to find the right product.

#1 waterproof jackets Poor ranking

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket' (0.852 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-relevance items like 'Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket' (0.633 relevance) and 'The North Face Mens Quest Hooded Jacket' (0.496 relevance) ranked #1 and #4 respectively. Multiple high-relevance Patagonia Torrentshell jackets (0.785-0.789 relevance) were scattered at positions #2, #8, and #13 instead of being grouped at the top.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Customer saw

#1 0.63

Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket £340.00

#2 0.79

Patagonia Mens Torrentshell 3L Rain Jacket 138 £

#3 0.70

The North Face Womens Dryvent Hikesteller Parka £160.00

#4 0.50

The North Face Mens Quest Hooded Jacket £115.00

#5 0.76

Rab Womens Downpour Jacket £120.00

Should have seen

#14 0.85

Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket £330.00

#2 0.79

Patagonia Mens Torrentshell 3L Rain Jacket 138 £

#8 0.79

Patagonia Womens Torrentshell 3L Jacket 119 £

#13 0.79

Patagonia Womens Torrentshell 3L Jacket 119 £

#5 0.76

Rab Womens Downpour Jacket £120.00

#2 water proof jackets Poor ranking

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket' (0.840 relevance) was buried at position #14, while much lower relevance results like 'Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket' (0.582 relevance) and 'The North Face Mens Quest Hooded Jacket' (0.469 relevance) were ranked #1 and #4 respectively. The engine successfully retrieved waterproof jackets but completely failed to rank them by relevance score.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#3 mountain equipment Poor ranking

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'Mountain Equipment Mens Nanda Devi Jacket' (0.840 relevance) was buried at position #12, while lower relevance items like 'Mountain Equipment Mens Ibex Mountain Trousers' (0.828 relevance) appeared at #1. The second-best result 'Mountain Equipment Mens Makalu Jacket' (0.832 relevance) was also misplaced at #11. Additionally, 'Mountain Equipment Helium 600 Sleeping Bag' (0.812 relevance) was pushed down to #15 despite having the same relevance as items ranked much higher.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#4 soft shell jackets Poor ranking

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The most relevant result 'Ayacucho Mens Mountain Light Softshell Jacket' (relevance: 0.832) was buried at position #11, while much less relevant results like 'The North Face Womens Dryvent Hikesteller Parka' (relevance: 0.508) and 'Mountain Equipment Mens Nanda Devi Jacket' (relevance: 0.488) occupied the top positions. The only product that actually contains 'Softshell' in its title was ranked 11th despite having the highest relevance score.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

#5 something waterproof for walking in the rain No semantic understanding

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The query 'something waterproof for walking in the rain' returned only 2 results: a promotional banner 'Mid-Season Sale - Up to 30% Off' at #1 and a 'no matching products' message at #2. Despite the query clearly describing waterproof outdoor gear, no actual waterproof jackets, rain gear, or walking equipment were retrieved.

Fix

Add synonym and semantic mappings for this customer phrasing so the engine recognizes the intended product need.

Customer saw

#1 0.31

Mid-Season Sale - Up to 30% Off

#2 0.81

We don't have matching products for: “something+waterproof+for+walking+in+the+rain”

Should have seen

#2 0.81

We don't have matching products for: “something+waterproof+for+walking+in+the+rain”

#1 0.31

Mid-Season Sale - Up to 30% Off

#6 jacket Poor ranking

The best matching result was not surfaced cleanly.

Evidence

The highest relevance result 'The North Face Mens Whiton 3L Jacket' (0.809) was buried at position #11, while lower-scoring results like 'Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket' (0.762) occupied positions #1 and #2. The top 3 positions contained results with relevance scores of 0.762, 0.762, and 0.801, while better matches with scores of 0.805 and 0.809 were pushed to positions #13 and #11 respectively.

Fix

Re-rank returned products so the strongest intent matches appear first, especially when the right products already exist in the result set.

Detailed Analysis

Product Discovery

5 examples
"waterproof jackets"
Direct match · Critical · Best result at position #1

The highest relevance result 'Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket' (0.852 relevance) was buried at position #14, while lower-relevance items like 'Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket' (0.633 relevance) and 'The North Face Mens Quest Hooded Jacket' (0.496 relevance) ranked #1 and #4 respectively. Multiple high-relevance Patagonia Torrentshell jackets (0.785-0.789 relevance) were scattered at positions #2, #8, and #13 instead of being grouped at the top.

Customer Saw

  1. Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket (£340.00)
  2. Patagonia Mens Torrentshell 3L Rain Jacket (138 £)
  3. The North Face Womens Dryvent Hikesteller Parka (£160.00)
  4. The North Face Mens Quest Hooded Jacket (£115.00)
  5. Rab Womens Downpour Jacket (£120.00)

Should Have Seen

  1. Mountain Equipment Womens Makalu Waterproof Jacket (£330.00)
  2. Patagonia Mens Torrentshell 3L Rain Jacket (138 £)
  3. Patagonia Womens Torrentshell 3L Jacket (119 £)
"jackets"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #1

The highest relevance result 'Patagonia Womens Torrentshell 3L Jacket' (0.766 relevance) was buried at position #13, while lower-scoring results like 'Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket' (0.734 relevance) occupied positions #1 and #2. Multiple high-scoring jackets were displaced: 'The North Face Mens Whiton 3L Jacket' (0.762) was at #11, and 'Arc'teryx Mens Atom Hooded Jacket' (0.758) was at #7.

Customer Saw

  1. Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
  2. Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
  3. Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket (£340.00)
  4. Rab Mens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
  5. Patagonia Mens Torrentshell 3L Rain Jacket (138 £)

Should Have Seen

  1. Patagonia Womens Torrentshell 3L Jacket (119 £)
  2. Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket (£340.00)
  3. The North Face Mens Whiton 3L Jacket (£185.00)
"footwear"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #1

The search engine correctly retrieved relevant footwear products but ranked them poorly. The highest relevance product 'Scarpa Mens Cyrus 2 GTX Shoes' (0.691 relevance) was buried at position #11, while two sock products with much lower relevance scores (0.465 and 0.473) occupied the top two positions. Multiple high-quality boots from Scarpa, Merrell, and other brands with relevance scores above 0.66 were pushed down to positions 7-14.

Customer Saw

  1. Bridgedale Mens Ultra Lightweight Merino Endurance Socks (208 £)
  2. Bridgedale Womens Ultra Lightweight Merino Endurance Socks (278 £)
  3. Danner Womens Mountain 600 Evo GTX Boots (1 £)
  4. Danner Mens Mountain 600 Evo GTX Boots (2 £)
  5. Salomon Womens Quest 5 GTX Boots (3 £)

Should Have Seen

  1. Scarpa Mens Cyrus 2 GTX Shoes (43 £)
  2. Scarpa Mens Terra GTX Boots (£210.00)
  3. Scarpa Womens Terra GTX Boots (£210.00)
"sale"
Broad category · Critical · Best result at position #1

When searching for 'sale', customers expect to see discounted products, but the engine returned full-price items like Meindl Mens Bhutan MFS GTX Boots at £290.00 (#3), Rab Mens Kangri GTX Jacket at £340.00 (#9), and multiple £210.00 jackets. The highest relevance item (Rab Kit Bag II - 80L at 0.531 relevance) was buried at position #11. None of the top results appear to be sale items despite this being a clear navigation query for the Sale category.

Customer Saw

  1. Bridgedale Mens Special Edition Merino Hiker Socks (£26.00)
  2. Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
  3. Meindl Mens Bhutan MFS GTX Boots (£290.00)
  4. Scarpa Mens Terra GTX Boots (£210.00)
  5. Scarpa Womens Terra GTX Boots (£210.00)

Should Have Seen

  1. Rab Kit Bag II - 80L (£130.00)
  2. Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
  3. Rab Womens Microlight Alpine Jacket (£210.00)
"walking boots"
Direct match · Moderate · Best result at position #1

Typo Tolerance

"patagnoia"
Typo · Critical · Best result at position #1

The search engine correctly handled the typo 'patagnoia' and retrieved all Patagonia products, but severely misranked them. The highest relevance result 'Patagonia Mens P6 Logo Responsibili- T-Shirt' (0.621 relevance) was buried at position #12, while lower-scoring items like 'Patagonia Womens Retro Pile Marsupial Fleece' (0.566 relevance) appeared at #1. Filters & Constraints

"patagonia fleeces sale"
Multi-attribute · Critical · Best result at position #1

The query 'patagonia fleeces sale' includes 'sale' as a constraint, but none of the returned results appear to be on sale. All products show regular prices (£94-£140) with no sale indicators. The highest relevance item 'Patagonia Womens Retro Pile Marsupial Fleece' at £140.00 was buried at position #15, while a lower-relevance item 'Patagonia Mens R1 Air Full-Zip Hoodie Jacket' at £94 was ranked #1, suggesting the engine may have confused lower price with sale status.

Positive Findings

What's Working

  • Retrieval is working — relevant results were found in 32 of 62 queries. The primary issue is ranking, not finding: the right results exist in the result set but aren't being surfaced at the top.
  • Close to correct on 62 queries ("waterproof jackets", "walking boots", "gore-tex jackets"): the best result was within the top 3, just not at #1.
Full Data

Scope and Boundaries

What this audit covers

Included

Search relevance, ranking quality, retrieval gaps, query interpretation failures, visible result ordering, and prioritized fixes based on the tested query set.

Not included

Implementation work, platform configuration changes, merchandising strategy, analytics instrumentation, or a full conversion-rate analysis beyond the search result experience.

Next Steps

We'd welcome the opportunity to walk you through these findings in detail and discuss a remediation plan tailored to your team's priorities and timeline.

The issues identified in this audit are fixable — most ecommerce search platforms support the capabilities tested here, and the roadmap above is ordered by impact to help you prioritize.

Schedule a 30-Minute Walkthrough
